review_comments: [' ## Merging this PR will **not alter performance** `✅ 20` untouched benchmarks --- Comparing garagon:fix/mount-subapp-under-router (fc21d83) with master (3b9d848)[^unexpected-base] [^unexpected-base]: No successful run was found on master (d128a70) during the generation of this report, so 3b9d848 was used instead as the comparison base. There might be some changes unrelated to this pull request in this report. ', '@garagon, thanks for your interest! Please, review existing PRs linked to the issue (including closed PRs, to understand the reasons they were closed) before opening your PRs. And provide an explanation why you think your approach is better. See https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/pull/13944', '@YuriiMotov thanks for the pointer. I reviewed all related PRs: - #13944 (yours, open): supports StaticFiles only, raises `FastAPIError` for other ASGI apps - #14032 (sujin502, closed): generic Mount support, closed for inactivity - #14924, #14926 (kimchikingdom, closed): generic Mount, you redirected to #13944 - #14793 (jonathan-fulton, open): generic Mount support - #14984 (fcarvajalbrown, closed), #15014 (Anandesh-Sharma, closed): same approach as ours I understand your position: sub-apps should be mounted to the root app, and only StaticFiles makes sense on a sub-router. Looking at the issue comments, there are real use cases beyond StaticFiles that users are requesting: - Mounting v1/v2 API versions under a shared prefix router - Serving Dash dashboards alongside API routes on the same router - Adding authentication dependencies to third-party ASGI apps via router - Modular app composition where teams own routers with their own sub-apps The current behavior silently drops Mount routes with no error and no warning. Your PR (#13944) solves this for StaticFiles and raises a clear error for everything else, which is better than silent failure. Our approach propagates all Mount routes with the correct prefix. The technical difference is one line: `self.mount(prefix + router.prefix + route.path, app=route.app, name=route.name)`. The reason `router.prefix` is needed is that `APIRoute.path` has the prefix baked in at creation time, but `Mount.path` does not. Both approaches are valid. If Sebastian decides StaticFiles-only is the right scope, #13944 is the right PR. If full Mount support is desired, the approach in this PR (and #14793) handles it. Happy to close this if #13944 is the direction the project wants to take.']

review:
[
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `include_router` 中处理 `Mount` 路由时，路径拼接逻辑存在错误。当 `router.prefix` 不为空时，代码将 `router.prefix` 重复拼接到了 `Mount` 的路径上。`Mount` 对象本身已经包含了其挂载路径 `route.path`，并且 `router.prefix` 已经在 `include_router` 的 `prefix` 参数中处理。直接加上 `router.prefix` 会导致最终路径错误，例如，如果 `router.prefix='/api'`，`Mount` 的 `route.path='/subapi'`，`include_router` 的 `prefix=''`，那么最终路径会变成 `/api/api/subapi` 而不是正确的 `/api/subapi`。这会导致挂载的子应用无法通过预期的URL访问。",
    "evidence": "文件 `fastapi/routing.py` 第1825行：`prefix + router.prefix + route.path`。根据项目背景，`APIRouter` 的 `prefix` 在 `include_router` 时通过 `prefix` 参数处理。`Mount` 的 `route.path` 是相对于路由器前缀的挂载点。",
    "advice": "应该只使用 `prefix + route.path` 来构建最终的挂载路径。`router.prefix` 不应在此处重复添加，因为它已经隐含在 `include_router` 的上下文中，或者通过 `prefix` 参数传递。建议修改为：`self.mount(prefix + route.path, app=route.app, name=route.name)`。",
    "confidence": 0.95,
    "severity": "High"
  },
  {
    "type": "Design",
    "description": "新增的 `Mount` 处理逻辑与项目现有的 `APIRouter` 设计模式不一致。在 FastAPI 中，`APIRouter` 主要用于组织路由端点（`APIRoute`, `WebSocketRoute`），而 `Mount` 用于挂载独立的 Starlette 应用。将 `Mount` 直接添加到 `APIRouter.routes` 中并在 `include_router` 中特殊处理，可能破坏了 `APIRouter` 作为纯路由容器的抽象。这可能导致未来维护的复杂性，例如在路由遍历、OpenAPI 文档生成等方面需要额外考虑 `Mount` 类型。",
    "evidence": "文件 `fastapi/routing.py` 新增的 `elif isinstance(route, routing.Mount):` 块。项目背景强调 FastAPI 基于 Starlette 并遵循清晰的设计模式。现有的 `APIRouter` 主要处理请求路由，而 `mount` 操作通常在顶级 `FastAPI` 应用上执行。",
    "advice": "建议重新评估此功能的设计。如果确实需要支持在路由器内挂载子应用，应考虑是否通过其他方式实现，例如在路由器上提供一个明确的 `mount` 方法，该方法将挂载操作委托给父应用，而不是将 `Mount` 对象混入路由列表。或者，在文档中明确说明此行为是实验性的，并可能在未来版本中调整。",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "Medium"
  },
  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "description": "新增的测试文件 `test_mount_subrouter.py` 中的测试函数命名和结构良好，但缺少对错误路径（例如上述路径拼接错误）的测试覆盖。测试仅验证了成功场景，没有验证当 `router.prefix` 和 `Mount` 路径以特定方式组合时，错误的路径拼接是否会导致失败。这降低了测试对代码变更的防护能力。",
    "evidence": "文件 `test_mount_subrouter.py` 中的三个测试函数 `test_mount_subapp_under_router`、`test_mount_subapp_docs`、`test_mount_multiple_subapps` 都只测试了请求成功（状态码200）的情况。",
    "advice": "建议增加测试用例，专门测试路径拼接的正确性。例如，可以测试当 `APIRouter(prefix=\"/api\")` 且 `mount(\"/subapi\", subapp)` 时，访问 `/api/subapi/...` 是否成功，并断言访问 `/api/api/subapi/...` 会失败（返回404）。这有助于捕获上述的路径拼接错误。",
    "confidence": 0.7,
    "severity": "Medium"
  }
]

[
  {
    "type": "Bug",

My understanding is: - #14952 adds only a regression test. - #14703 moves resolution later via `get_type_hints(..., include_extras=True)` in `get_dependant()`, but the exact reproducer still failed there. - #14872 and #15142 try to resolve `ForwardRef` later in the pipeline, but in this bug the outer `Annotated[...]` structure has already been lost too early, so FastAPI never extracts `Depends(...)` correctly. - #15131 addresses the later OpenAPI failure in the Pydantic compat layer, but it does not fix the earlier dependency-extraction problem and changes a much broader area. - #14557 is closer, but it relies on manual parsing of `Annotated[...]` strings plus substring `eval`, which felt heavier and more fragile than necessary. - #15096 is the closest implementation-wise. Instead of keeping the whole annotation unresolved, or converting missing inner names to `Any`, this PR evaluates with a lenient namespace that preserves unresolved inner names as nested `ForwardRef(...)` objects. That means FastAPI still sees the outer `Annotated[...]` structure, can still extract `Depends(...)`, and does not lose the original type information unnecessarily. I think that is better because: - it is a smaller and more local change - it fixes both runtime dependency extraction and OpenAPI generation from the same root cause - it avoids special-case parsing of `Annotated[...]` - it preserves unresolved types as `ForwardRef(...)` instead of degrading them to `Any` I also added a focused regression test for the exact issue shape from #13056, covering both runtime behavior and OpenAPI generation. ']

See https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/pull/13944', '> @abishekgiri, thanks for your interest! > > Please, review existing PRs linked to the issue (including closed PRs, to understand the reasons they were closed) before opening your PRs. And provide an explanation why you think your approach is better. > > See #13944 I reviewed the related PRs: #13944, #14793, #14032, #14924, #14926, #14984, and #15014. My understanding is that the closed PRs were mostly closed for one of two reasons: - they were partial fixes that only handled `Mount` propagation in `include_router()` - or the discussion shifted toward a `StaticFiles`-only scope (`#13944`) rather than general mounted ASGI apps That is why I chose this approach. The include-only fixes (`#14032`, `#14984`, `#15014`) solve one symptom, but they do not fix the underlying inconsistency: `APIRouter.mount()` still does not apply the router prefix when the `Mount` is created. In contrast, this PR fixes both pieces together: 1. `APIRouter.mount()` applies the router prefix when creating the mount 2. `include_router()` preserves `Mount` routes instead of silently dropping them I think that is cleaner because it makes `Mount` behave more like other route types: the route is normalized when it is added, and then inclusion just carries it forward. Compared with `#13944`, this PR is intentionally broader. `#13944` is the right PR if the project decision is to support only `StaticFiles` on sub-routers and reject other ASGI apps. My PR instead keeps the existing `APIRouter.mount()` API working for general mounted sub-applications. Compared with `#14793`, the core implementation is very close, so I do not want to overstate a difference there. The main extra value I tried to add here is regression coverage for mounted sub-app OpenAPI behavior, since the original issue also mentioned docs/spec URLs not working as expected. So my view is: - if the intended direction is `StaticFiles`-only, then `#13944` is the correct direction - if the intent is to make the existing `APIRouter.mount()` API work for general mounted sub-applications, then I think the two-part fix in this PR is the right approach If you prefer consolidating on `#14793` instead, I’m also fine with that. ']

My reasoning for this approach is: - `#13861`, `#14881`, and `#15070` make the selected method deterministic, but they do not solve the duplicate `operationId` problem itself. - `#15074` appends the current method to `route.unique_id`, which already contains a method suffix in the default generator, so it can produce IDs like `..._post_post`. - `#15140` and `#15194` are closer, but they still have to reverse-engineer a base ID from `route.unique_id`. That is brittle for custom `generate_unique_id_function` values, especially `#15140`, which strips everything after the last underscore. - `#15215` rebuilds the base from `route.name + route.path_format` inside OpenAPI, which bypasses `generate_unique_id_function` for multi-method routes. I chose this implementation because it fixes the root ambiguity instead of patching around it later: - for multi-method routes, the default `unique_id` becomes a shared base - the per-method suffix is added only when emitting OpenAPI - explicit `operation_id` remains unchanged - custom `generate_unique_id_function` is still respected, because OpenAPI starts from `route.unique_id` instead of reconstructing it I think that is cleaner because `route.unique_id` is reused internally beyond `operationId`, so removing the arbitrary “first method” from the canonical route ID is more robust than preserving it and then trying to peel it back off in OpenAPI generation. [{'sha': 'c5bd35c728c32fc7aa28fd650e7673678dd9d3a1', 'message': 'fix: treat empty bearer token as missing credentials in OAuth2 helpers

Authorization: Bearer  (empty token after "Bearer") was returned as ""
instead of triggering the auto_error path. Both OAuth2PasswordBearer and
OAuth2AuthorizationCodeBearer only checked that the scheme was "bearer"
but not that the credential string was non-empty.

RFC 6750 Section 2.1 defines b64token = 1*(...) — at least one character
required. An empty credential is syntactically malformed and should be
treated the same as missing credentials: 401 with auto_error=True, None
with auto_error=False.

Fix: add `not param` to the guard condition in both __call__ methods.

Closes: https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/discussions/15192'}, {'sha': 'ca96fdf2922f0d08bfe468c78024ccedfa6fb937', 'message': "Merge branch 'master' into fix/oauth2-empty-bearer"}]
review_comments: ['## Pull request overview This PR fixes OAuth2 bearer-token extraction so that an empty bearer credential (`Authorization: Bearer `) is treated as missing credentials, triggering the `auto_error` behavior instead of returning an empty string token. **Changes:** - Update `OAuth2PasswordBearer.__call__` and `OAuth2AuthorizationCodeBearer.__call__` to treat empty `param` as unauthenticated. - Add regression tests covering empty bearer token handling for both optional (`auto_error=False`) and required (`auto_error=True`) OAuth2 helpers. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `fastapi/security/oauth2.py` | Adds `or not param` guard so empty bearer credentials follow the unauthenticated path. | | `tests/test_security_oauth2_password_bearer_optional.py` | Adds a test asserting empty bearer token behaves like missing credentials when `auto_error=False`. | | `tests/test_security_oauth2_authorization_code_bearer.py` | Adds a test asserting empty bearer token triggers 401 when `auto_error=True`. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.', 'based on the discussion https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/discussions/15192', ' ## Merging this PR will **not alter performance** `✅ 20` untouched benchmarks --- Comparing subhashdasyam:fix/oauth2-empty-bearer (ca96fdf) with master (8238446)[^unexpected-base] [^unexpected-base]: No successful run was found on master (937d307) during the generation of this report, so 8238446 was used instead as the comparison base. There might be some changes unrelated to this pull request in this report. ']

[{'sha': '7c4b6412438d1059f623a637e8ebc7efc6b29694', 'message': 'Fix duplicate operationId for multi-method routes

When a route is registered with multiple HTTP methods via
add_api_route(..., methods=["POST", "DELETE"]), generate_unique_id()
only uses the first method from the set, causing all methods to share
the same unique_id and producing duplicate operationIds in the OpenAPI
schema along with a UserWarning.

Fix by detecting multi-method routes in get_openapi_operation_metadata
and building a per-method operationId using the current method parameter
(already available in the function), following the same pattern as
generate_unique_id.

Fixes #13175'}, {'sha': 'b2e6f8dc1ec9bc1af69e4b8264829f79cd9eb98a', 'message': 'Add test for unique operationId generation in multi-method routes'}, {'sha': '937fcdffbeef192f8236442ed098dc768ab0696d', 'message': '🎨 Auto format'}]
review_comments: [' ## Merging this PR will **not alter performance** `✅ 20` untouched benchmarks --- Comparing fitratgulmamadov:fix/issue-13175-duplicate-operation-id (937fcdf) with master (8238446)[^unexpected-base] [^unexpected-base]: No successful run was found on master (937d307) during the generation of this report, so 8238446 was used instead as the comparison base. There might be some changes unrelated to this pull request in this report. ', '@fitratgulmamadov, have you reviewed other PRs linked to that issue? Please, describe why this approach is better', "> @fitratgulmamadov, have you reviewed other PRs linked to that issue? Please, describe why this approach is better Yes, I reviewed the linked PRs before submitting. **Why this approach differs from rejected PRs:** - **#15070**: Only sorted methods for determinism, didn't make IDs unique per method — not a real fix. - **#15074 / #15140**: Appended method to `route.unique_id`, producing double suffixes like `do_something__put_put`. Our approach rebuilds the base from `route.name + route.path_format` using the same pattern as `generate_unique_id`, so the result is clean: `do_something__put` and `do_something__delete`. - **#10694**: Modifies routing layer. Our fix is more minimal — it stays in `get_openapi_operation_metadata()` where `method` is already available, touching only the OpenAPI generation step. **Known limitation**: The body schema name (`Body_do_something__put`) is still generated at route compilation time and remains non-deterministic for multi-method routes. This would require a separate fix at the routing layer (as #10694 attempts). Our PR addresses only the operationId duplication. Happy to adjust the approach based on your feedback. "]

[{'sha': 'a1a7e1f7b65c59f3e1fea97eb927c9a6b2cdd0a9', 'message': 'fix(routing): thread stream_item_type through include_router()

When a typed SSE or JSONL streaming endpoint is registered via a router,
the OpenAPI schema loses the stream item type because include_router()
re-creates routes via add_api_route() without passing stream_item_type.

This fix threads stream_item_type through add_api_route() and include_router(),
following the strict_content_type pattern.

Fixes #15077'}]

[{'sha': 'a1a7e1f7b65c59f3e1fea97eb927c9a6b2cdd0a9', 'message': 'fix(routing): thread stream_item_type through include_router()

When a typed SSE or JSONL streaming endpoint is registered via a router,
the OpenAPI schema loses the stream item type because include_router()
re-creates routes via add_api_route() without passing stream_item_type.

This fix threads stream_item_type through add_api_route() and include_router(),
following the strict_content_type pattern.

Fixes #15077'}]

The documentation incorrectly stated that clients could send None as
a query parameter value. In reality, omitting a required parameter
returns 422, and there is no standard HTTP mechanism to pass None
as a query string value.

Changes:
- Updated docs to clarify that None in the type annotation means
  the OpenAPI schema marks the parameter as nullable
- Added info box explaining the actual behavior
- Fixed xfail tests to verify correct 422 behavior instead of
  expecting impossible 200 responses

Closes #12419"}]

[{'sha': '11dc2808067acb71771729205f124570d7205835', 'message': "Fix duplicate operation IDs for routes with multiple methods

When a route is registered with multiple HTTP methods (e.g., methods=['POST', 'DELETE']),
the default generate_unique_id function only uses the first method, causing duplicate
operation IDs in the OpenAPI schema.

This fix ensures that when a route has multiple methods and no explicit operation_id,
each method gets a unique operation ID by appending the current method name.

Fixes #13175"}]

commits:
[{'sha': '8bfab526a2c015765fb708dd2c63d816a4461edb', 'message': 'fix: reject newline characters in SSE event and id fields

`format_sse_event()` interpolated the `event` and `id` fields into SSE
wire-format bytes without stripping or rejecting newline characters. A
`\n` or `\r\n` in either field injects an additional SSE field-line into
the stream — enabling event-type spoofing and fabricated data payloads
in browser EventSource clients.

The `data` and `comment` fields were already protected via `splitlines()`;
the omission for `event` and `id` was an inconsistency, not a design
decision. The identical bug class was fixed in Hono (GHSA-p6xx-57qc-3wxr).

Changes:
- Add `_check_event_no_newline()` validator and apply it to
  `ServerSentEvent.event` via `AfterValidator`
- Extend `_check_id_no_null()` to also reject `\n` and `\r`
- Strip `\r`/`\n` in `format_sse_event()` for `event` and `id` as
  defense-in-depth (low-level function, bypasses model validators)
- Add 7 regression tests covering LF/CR rejection in both the model
  and the low-level formatting function

Severity: Medium (client-side injection; server unaffected)
CWE: CWE-116 — Improper Encoding for Output in a Different Plaintext Context
OWASP: A03:2021 — Injection
Prior art: Hono GHSA-p6xx-57qc-3wxr (same class, same fix pattern)'}, {'sha': '06eccbf077b76309382371fe9440083bea350d54', 'message': "fix: add null-byte protection to SSE 'event' field for symmetry with 'id'

`_check_event_no_newline` lacked a `\0` check that `_check_id_no_null_or_newline`
has had since the original implementation. Similarly, `format_sse_event()` stripped
`\0` from `id` but not from `event`.

Changes:
- Extend `_check_event_no_newline` to also reject `\0` characters
- Strip `\0` in `format_sse_event()` for `event` (mirrors existing `id` behavior)
- Add `test_server_sent_event_null_event_rejected` regression test

`event` and `id` are now fully symmetric in both the model validator and the
low-level wire formatter."}, {'sha': '9e51a7d6956376104e9ca31a09322083671be9a2', 'message': "fix: raise ValueError in format_sse_event instead of stripping invalid chars

Replace silent .replace() sanitization in format_sse_event() with explicit
ValueError raises for \n, \r, and \0 in the event and id fields.

Previously, format_sse_event() would strip those characters, creating a
behavioral inconsistency with ServerSentEvent's AfterValidator which raises
on the same input. As YuriiMotov pointed out: data allows newlines by SSE
spec design (each line becomes a separate data: frame), but event and id are
single-token fields where a newline is always a protocol violation. Silent
stripping masks bugs at the call site; raising surfaces them where they belong.

Both code paths now fail identically for the same invalid input. Tests updated
from strip-behavior assertions to ValueError assertions, plus two new null-byte
tests covering format_sse_event() directly for event and id."}]
review_comments: ['`_check_id_no_null` now validates both NUL and newline/CR characters, so the function name is misleading. Consider renaming it to reflect all rejected characters (e.g., `_check_id_no_null_or_newline`) to avoid future callers assuming it only checks for `\0`.', '`format_sse_event()` now strips both `\n` and `\r` from the `id` field, but the new tests only assert stripping for `\n`. Add a regression test for `id` containing `\r` (similar to the existing event CR test) so both branches of the new sanitization are covered. ```suggestion def test_format_sse_event_strips_cr_in_id(): from fastapi.sse import format_sse_event result = format_sse_event(id="42\rpwned", data_str="hello") assert b"\rpwned" not in result assert b"id: 42pwned\n" in result ```', '`format_sse_event()` sanitizes `id` for CR/LF now, but it still allows `\0` even though `ServerSentEvent.id` rejects it via `_check_id_no_null`. Since `format_sse_event()` is a low-level API callable without Pydantic validation, consider also rejecting or stripping NULs here to keep behavior consistent and avoid emitting spec-invalid `id:` lines. ```suggestion id = id.replace("\r", "").replace("\n", "").replace("\0", "") ```', 'I find it strange to have these `replace` here. This looks like an alternative to validation logic implemented using `AfterValidator`. @subhashdasyam, could you please clarify your intention?', 'The `replace` calls in `format_sse_event()` are not an alternative to the validator. They cover a different code path. `format_sse_event()` is a public low-level function. The routing layer in `routing.py` calls it directly with pre-validated fields from a `ServerSentEvent`, so in that path the sanitization is a no-op. But `format_sse_event()` can also be called without ever constructing a `ServerSentEvent`, for example from custom integrations or internal tooling that builds SSE frames directly. In that path there is no Pydantic validator in play at all. The intent is two layers with different jobs: the `AfterValidator` is a strict gate at the model boundary, and the `replace` calls in `format_sse_event()` are a safety net for callers who skip the model. This follows the same pattern as the existing `splitlines()` on `data` and `comment`. Those fields have no Pydantic validator, but the function still protects the wire format regardless. The follow-up commit from the comment I received from @SadManFahIm also extended this pattern to cover `\0` for `event`, fixing an asymmetry with `id` that was pointed out in review. Both layers now handle the same character set consistently, which is another sign the two layers serve different purposes rather than duplicating each other.', "@subhashdasyam These aren't duplicating each other — they cover different call paths. The routing layer always goes through a ServerSentEvent, so the AfterValidator fires and the replace calls become a no-op there. In that path there's no Pydantic validation at all, so the replace calls are the only protection. On point 1 -- bare `\r` test coverage is already there. `test_server_sent_event_cr_event_rejected` at line 229 covers exactly this case: ```python def test_server_sent_event_cr_event_rejected(): with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="newline"): ServerSentEvent(data="test", event="chat\rpwned") ``` A bare `\r` without `\n`, asserts a `ValueError` is raised. Same pattern exists for `id` in `test_server_sent_event_cr_id_rejected`.', "I don't think `format_sse_event` should be treated as a part of public API as it's not documented and seems to be for internal use only. But yes, not having this guard logic in `format_sse_event` may lead to introducing bugs in the future. Anyway, I believe we should be consistent in how we handle this. commits:
[{'sha': '8bfab526a2c015765fb708dd2c63d816a4461edb', 'message': 'fix: reject newline characters in SSE event and id fields

`format_sse_event()` interpolated the `event` and `id` fields into SSE
wire-format bytes without stripping or rejecting newline characters. A
`\n` or `\r\n` in either field injects an additional SSE field-line into
the stream — enabling event-type spoofing and fabricated data payloads
in browser EventSource clients.

The `data` and `comment` fields were already protected via `splitlines()`;
the omission for `event` and `id` was an inconsistency, not a design
decision. The identical bug class was fixed in Hono (GHSA-p6xx-57qc-3wxr).

Changes:
- Add `_check_event_no_newline()` validator and apply it to
  `ServerSentEvent.event` via `AfterValidator`
- Extend `_check_id_no_null()` to also reject `\n` and `\r`
- Strip `\r`/`\n` in `format_sse_event()` for `event` and `id` as
  defense-in-depth (low-level function, bypasses model validators)
- Add 7 regression tests covering LF/CR rejection in both the model
  and the low-level formatting function

Severity: Medium (client-side injection; server unaffected)
CWE: CWE-116 — Improper Encoding for Output in a Different Plaintext Context
OWASP: A03:2021 — Injection
Prior art: Hono GHSA-p6xx-57qc-3wxr (same class, same fix pattern)'}, {'sha': '06eccbf077b76309382371fe9440083bea350d54', 'message': "fix: add null-byte protection to SSE 'event' field for symmetry with 'id'

`_check_event_no_newline` lacked a `\0` check that `_check_id_no_null_or_newline`
has had since the original implementation. Similarly, `format_sse_event()` stripped
`\0` from `id` but not from `event`.

Changes:
- Extend `_check_event_no_newline` to also reject `\0` characters
- Strip `\0` in `format_sse_event()` for `event` (mirrors existing `id` behavior)
- Add `test_server_sent_event_null_event_rejected` regression test

`event` and `id` are now fully symmetric in both the model validator and the
low-level wire formatter."}, {'sha': '9e51a7d6956376104e9ca31a09322083671be9a2', 'message': "fix: raise ValueError in format_sse_event instead of stripping invalid chars

Replace silent .replace() sanitization in format_sse_event() with explicit
ValueError raises for \n, \r, and \0 in the event and id fields.

Previously, format_sse_event() would strip those characters, creating a
behavioral inconsistency with ServerSentEvent's AfterValidator which raises
on the same input. As YuriiMotov pointed out: data allows newlines by SSE
spec design (each line becomes a separate data: frame), but event and id are
single-token fields where a newline is always a protocol violation. Silent
stripping masks bugs at the call site; raising surfaces them where they belong.

Both code paths now fail identically for the same invalid input. Tests updated
from strip-behavior assertions to ValueError assertions, plus two new null-byte
tests covering format_sse_event() directly for event and id."}]
review_comments: ['`_check_id_no_null` now validates both NUL and newline/CR characters, so the function name is misleading. Consider renaming it to reflect all rejected characters (e.g., `_check_id_no_null_or_newline`) to avoid future callers assuming it only checks for `\0`.', '`format_sse_event()` now strips both `\n` and `\r` from the `id` field, but the new tests only assert stripping for `\n`. Add a regression test for `id` containing `\r` (similar to the existing event CR test) so both branches of the new sanitization are covered. ```suggestion def test_format_sse_event_strips_cr_in_id(): from fastapi.sse import format_sse_event result = format_sse_event(id="42\rpwned", data_str="hello") assert b"\rpwned" not in result assert b"id: 42pwned\n" in result ```', '`format_sse_event()` sanitizes `id` for CR/LF now, but it still allows `\0` even though `ServerSentEvent.id` rejects it via `_check_id_no_null`. Since `format_sse_event()` is a low-level API callable without Pydantic validation, consider also rejecting or stripping NULs here to keep behavior consistent and avoid emitting spec-invalid `id:` lines. ```suggestion id = id.replace("\r", "").replace("\n", "").replace("\0", "") ```', 'I find it strange to have these `replace` here. This looks like an alternative to validation logic implemented using `AfterValidator`. @subhashdasyam, could you please clarify your intention?', 'The `replace` calls in `format_sse_event()` are not an alternative to the validator. They cover a different code path. `format_sse_event()` is a public low-level function. The routing layer in `routing.py` calls it directly with pre-validated fields from a `ServerSentEvent`, so in that path the sanitization is a no-op. But `format_sse_event()` can also be called without ever constructing a `ServerSentEvent`, for example from custom integrations or internal tooling that builds SSE frames directly. In that path there is no Pydantic validator in play at all. The intent is two layers with different jobs: the `AfterValidator` is a strict gate at the model boundary, and the `replace` calls in `format_sse_event()` are a safety net for callers who skip the model. This follows the same pattern as the existing `splitlines()` on `data` and `comment`. Those fields have no Pydantic validator, but the function still protects the wire format regardless. The follow-up commit from the comment I received from @SadManFahIm also extended this pattern to cover `\0` for `event`, fixing an asymmetry with `id` that was pointed out in review. Both layers now handle the same character set consistently, which is another sign the two layers serve different purposes rather than duplicating each other.', "@subhashdasyam These aren't duplicating each other — they cover different call paths. Same pattern exists for `id` in `test_server_sent_event_cr_id_rejected`.', "I don't think `format_sse_event` should be treated as a part of public API as it's not documented and seems to be for internal use only. The trouble with having `format_sse_event` construct a `ServerSentEvent` internally is that the two APIs don't share the same data model. `format_sse_event` takes `data_str` (already serialized data) while `ServerSentEvent` takes `data: Any` and `raw_data: str | None`. There's no clean mapping between them. On top of that, the routing layer calls `format_sse_event` with fields it already pulled from a validated `ServerSentEvent`, so you'd be validating the same thing twice. That seems like the wrong direction. That said, your consistency point stands. If `ServerSentEvent` raises on bad input, `format_sse_event` should too, not silently strip. commits:
[{'sha': '2ea91122c9960de437498a5c7ac83cca4a064cfc', 'message': 'Emit UserWarning when Response.background silently discards injected BackgroundTasks

When an endpoint injects BackgroundTasks via dependency injection AND
returns a Response that already has its own `background` attribute set,
the injected tasks are silently dropped. This is a confusing footgun
that causes tasks to disappear without any indication.

This change adds a UserWarning in that scenario so the silent data loss
becomes visible. The warning message explains how to resolve the
conflict (either use the injected BackgroundTasks exclusively, or stop
injecting BackgroundTasks when using Response.background directly).

Ref: https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/issues/11215

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-opus-4-6) <noreply@anthropic.com>'}, {'sha': 'cb0c5f89afdc062531bf68ab471c141d2ac57f0d', 'message': '🎨 Auto format'}]

review_comments:
['Added "no cover" because this line is only used for older versions of Pydantic. Ideally, I'd love to add a test configuration to test Pydantic 2.7.0 instead on the CI, to ensure we never break older, supported versions, cf "open question" in PR description ☝️ ', "LGTM, but **note that this change is breaking**. --- Alternatively we can probably do something like (in `encoders.py`): ```py try: from pydantic.color import Color # ty: ignore[deprecated] except ImportError: # pragma: no cover class Color: pass try: from pydantic_extra_types.color import Color as PyExtraColor except ImportError: # pragma: no cover class PyExtraColor: pass ... ENCODERS_BY_TYPE: dict[type[Any], Callable[[Any], Any]] = { bytes: lambda o: o.decode(), Color: str, # ty: ignore[deprecated] PyExtraColor: str, ... ``` (I haven't checked it) This way we support both `Color` implementations ", 'LGTM Just a note: this way we lose type hints for `evaluate_forwardref`. Not sure if it's important. As an alternative, we can try something like: ``` try: from pydantic._internal._typing_extra import try_eval_type except: try_eval_type = None try: from pydantic._internal._typing_extra import eval_type_lenient except: eval_type_lenient = None def evaluate_forwardref( value: Any, globalns: GlobalsNamespace | None = None, localns: MappingNamespace | None = None, ) -> Any: if try_eval_type is not None: return try_eval_type(value, globalns, localns)[0] if eval_type_lenient is not None: return eval_type_lenient(value, globalns, localns) raise RuntimeError("Both try_eval_type and eval_type_lenient are None") # pragma: no cover # Should never happen ``` But not sure it's worth it', 'I investigated this. Currently tests don't run on lowest Pydantic version (2.7) because of `pydantic-ai` optional dependency. `uv` fails to understand that this dependency is optional and considers it when resolving dependencies. So, as `pydantic-ai` depends on `pydantic 2.12`, it installs `pydantic 2.12`. I didn't manage to find a way to solve this.
``` [project] name = "test_resolution" version = "0.1.0" description = "Add your description here" readme = "README.md" requires-python = ">=3.10" dependencies = [ "pydantic>=2.7.0", ] [dependency-groups] translations = [ "pydantic-ai>=1.68.0", # Requires Pydantic 2.12 ] ``` `uv sync --no-dev --resolution lowest-direct` This will install pydantic 2.12 (even though `pydantic-ai` is not installed). Comment `pydantic-ai` dependency and it will install `pydantic` 2.7 (you will need to delete `uv.lock`)
So, maybe for now we can just manually add such configuration in test matrix?', 'For `pydantic_extra_types.color`, this will fail on `master` with > ValueError: [TypeError("'Color' object is not iterable"), TypeError('vars() argument must have __dict__ attribute')] ', "Thanks for catching this @YuriiMotov! You're totally right. I commited your suggestion and added a unit test that fails on `master`, so we now support both `Color` classes explicitely.", 'Separated this into its own PR: #15139', "I do think it's an improvement to have the types here explicitely. `GlobalsNamespace` and `MappingNamespace` are typing variants of `dict[str, Any]` and `Mapping[str, Any]` respectively, defined in [_namespace_utils.py](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic/blob/main/pydantic/_internal/_namespace_utils.py). This file only exists since Pydantic 2.10 though. In Pydantic 2.9, `eval_type_lenient` was typed twice with `dict[str, Any]`. So I've opted here to use `dict[str, Any]` as well, to support all Pydantic versions and not requireing external imports and more if/else logic. What do you think?", "As an alternative, we can use `try...except`: ```py try: return _pydantic_typing_extra.try_eval_type(value, globalns, localns)[0] except AttrbuteError: return _pydantic_typing_extra.eval_type_lenient( # ty: ignore[deprecated] value, globalns, localns ) # pragma: no cover ``` Or move this to module level, and only use it inside `evaluate_forwardref` (so that `evaluate_forwardref` would make it typed). It might also be a bit faster (but it's only used at app stratup, so maybe not as important)", '> For pydantic_extra_types.color, this will fail on master with > > ValueError: [TypeError("'Color' object is not iterable"), TypeError('vars() argument must have dict attribute')] > I took me some time to understand this comment :sweat_smile: Just to clarify if somebody also gets confused: it fails on `master` because `pydantic_extra_types.color.Color` is not currently supported by `jsonable_encoder`. This PR adds such support', "Yea I think it's a style preference, I'm personally more fan of checking explicitely first rather than relying on a `try`-`except`, but both are valid options. I'll leave it up to Sebastián to decide if he's got a preference 🙂 ", 'Great work @svlandeg! Clean approach to handling both deprecations 👍 A couple of thoughts: 1. **`Color` scoping** — Since `Color` is only used in `encoders.py` for the `ENCODERS_BY_TYPE` dict, the try/except fallback could live directly there instead of routing through `_compat/__init__.py`. That keeps the compat layer focused on core Pydantic abstractions (model fields, type evaluation, schema generation) rather than becoming a catch-all for deprecated imports. Minor though. 2. **Optional dep risk** — `pydantic-extra-types` is an optional dependency (under `standard`/`all`), so the `pydantic.color.Color` fallback is genuinely needed. Worth a small TODO comment noting that if Pydantic removes `pydantic.color` entirely in a future major, this fallback breaks silently. 3. **Success boolean from `try_eval_type`** — The lambda correctly discards it (matching `eval_type_lenient` behavior), but worth noting as a potential follow-up: `get_typed_annotation()` in `dependencies/utils.py` could use that boolean for better error messages when ForwardRef resolution fails silently. 4. **Naming nit** — `define_forwardref()` reads like it defines a forward ref, something like `_get_forwardref_evaluator()` might be clearer about what it actually does (selects the right eval function). Very minor. None of these are blockers — the core logic is solid, the `try_eval_type` wrapper is correct, and CI is all green. Really like the idea of proactively addressing these before `ty` enforcement lands. The open question about adding a Pydantic 2.7.0 CI run is a good one too — the `lowest-direct` matrix entry might already cover it, worth verifying. LGTM! 🚀', 'LGTM! review_comments: ["Here (and in a few similar cases), I've moved the typing to the var on the `class`, assuming that it wouldn't get a different type at any time. By moving the typing in this way, type checkers can more easily deduce the correct type of class vars.", 'Should we follow the deprecation recommendations and use `try_eval_type` directly? https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic/blob/938e81de2100b278f835d6e054bb7ecd58bcaff0/pydantic/_internal/_typing_extra.py#L445-L455', 'We should probably address this deprecation warning. I would actually introduce a breaking change and replace this import with `from pydantic_extra_types.color import Color` (maybe not in this PR).', 'We can probably try replacing this with [`Default(None)`](https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/blob/11614be9021aa4ac078d4d0693a8b5250a1010d8/fastapi/datastructures.py#L174)', "I think we should resolve this. Probably by updating the type hint of `solve_dependencies`'s `body` parameter to `Any` (or union of all possible types) and handling this inside", 'I think we should address this, not just ignore (class instance with `__call__` method will not have `__name__` attribute, but it will still be `Callable`): ``` dep_name = getattr(dependant.call, "__name__", repr(dependant.call)) raise DependencyScopeError( f'The dependency "{dep_name}" has a scope of ' '"request", it cannot depend on dependencies with scope "function".' ) ``` ', "```suggestion if isinstance(received_params, (ImmutableMultiDict, Headers)): value = received_params.getlist(key) ``` As it's done in `_get_multidict_value`", 'Same as https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/pull/15091/changes#r2919360642 ?', '```suggestion websocket_request_validation_exception_handler, # type: ignore[arg-type] # ty: ignore[unused-ignore-comment] ) # ty: ignore[no-matching-overload] ``` I think we should try to avoid too broad ignore comments', '```suggestion f: Callable[[Request], Awaitable[Response]] = ( func # type: ignore[assignment] # ty: ignore[unused-ignore-comment] if is_async_callable(func) else functools.partial(run_in_threadpool, func) # type: ignore[call-arg] # ty: ignore[unused-ignore-comment] ) # ty: ignore[invalid-assignment] ``` I think we should specify rules to ignore', 'I thought about that, but it would make us incompatible with older v2 versions. Instead I think if we want to address this deprecation, we should do it in a separate PR and by properly supporting all versions in the `compat` module.', 'I agree, see previous comment (we could do both in a separate PR)', 'Yes, totally agree!', 'cf https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/pull/15101 🙂 ', 'cf https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/pull/15101 🙂 ', 'done, cf [this commit](https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/pull/15091/commits/e6378e844e7a8cd82473a23b714ac979361132a3)', 'Like [this](https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/pull/15091/changes/8f896037945e6d22019fff6845c44290455461fc)?', 'cf [this commit](https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/pull/15091/changes/b2798dab90507ce04b98233c21fd77420061d989)', 'Great idea! see [this commit](https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/pull/15091/changes/11a0f1bf3cd3c7c5b4c0e401347481676111e2cc)', 'LGTM in general! Added several suggestions in review comments. Can help with the implementation if needed) @svlandeg, what do you think?', 'This looks awesome, thank you @svlandeg ! 🙌 And thanks @YuriiMotov for the thorough review too!', ' ## Merging this PR will **not alter performance** `✅ 20` untouched benchmarks --- Comparing svlandeg:feat/ty (11a0f1b) with master (4b82602)[^unexpected-base] [^unexpected-base]: No successful run was found on master (04b279f) during the generation of this report, so 4b82602 was used instead as the comparison base. [
  {
    "type": "Design",
    "description": "新增的 `fastapi_cli_installed` 装饰器使用了 `fastapi.cli.cli_main is None` 作为判断条件，这可能与项目已有的逻辑或约定不一致。在 FastAPI 项目中，通常使用 `importlib.util.find_spec` 或检查 `fastapi.cli` 模块的特定属性来检测可选依赖的安装情况。直接检查 `cli_main` 是否为 `None` 可能不够健壮，因为 `cli_main` 可能被定义为其他值（如一个函数），或者模块导入失败时可能引发 `AttributeError` 而非返回 `None`。这可能导致测试跳过逻辑不稳定。",
    "evidence": "+fastapi_cli_installed = pytest.mark.skipif(\n+    fastapi.cli.cli_main is None,\n+    reason=\"fastapi-cli is not installed\",\n+)",
    "advice": "建议采用更健壮的方式来检测 `fastapi-cli` 是否安装。例如，可以使用 `importlib.util.find_spec(\"fastapi_cli\")` 或 `try: import fastapi_cli; except ImportError: ...` 的模式。这符合 Python 社区检查可选依赖的常见做法，并与项目其他部分可能存在的类似检查保持一致。",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "Medium"
  },
  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "description": "新增的装饰器变量名 `fastapi_cli_installed` 具有误导性。该变量是一个 `pytest.mark.skipif` 装饰器，其语义是“如果 fastapi-cli 未安装则跳过”。然而，变量名 `fastapi_cli_installed` 字面意思是“fastapi-cli 已安装”，这与装饰器的实际行为（跳过未安装的情况）相反，容易造成理解上的混淆。",
    "evidence": "+fastapi_cli_installed = pytest.mark.skipif(\n+    fastapi.cli.cli_main is None,\n+    reason=\"fastapi-cli is not installed\",\n+)",
    "advice": "建议将变量名重命名为更能反映其用途的名称，例如 `skip_if_no_fastapi_cli` 或 `requires_fastapi_cli`。这将提高代码的可读性和可维护性，使其他开发者一眼就能理解该装饰器的意图。",
    "confidence": 0.9,
    "severity": "Low"
  }
]

    "description": "在 `get_discussions_experts` 函数中，新增了对 `comment.minimizedReason` 和 `reply.minimizedReason` 的检查，以排除某些原因被最小化的评论。然而，代码中使用的 `MINIMIZED_COMMENTS_REASONS_TO_EXCLUDE` 集合包含字符串 `\"abuse\"`、`\"off-topic\"`、`\"duplicate\"`、`\"spam\"`。这些是 GitHub 评论最小化的可能原因。但是，GitHub GraphQL API 返回的 `minimizedReason` 字段值可能是大写的（例如 `\"ABUSE\"`、`\"OFF_TOPIC\"`）。新增的 `LowerStr` 类型使用 `BeforeValidator` 将字符串转换为小写，这确保了比较时大小写不敏感。然而，`MINIMIZED_COMMENTS_REASONS_TO_EXCLUDE` 集合中的值是小写的，而 `LowerStr` 转换后的值也是小写的，因此比较应该有效。但是，存在一个潜在问题：GitHub API 可能返回 `\"OFF_TOPIC\"`（带下划线），而集合中是 `\"off-topic\"`（带连字符）。这可能导致检查失败，从而意外地包含应排除的评论。",
    "evidence": "`MINIMIZED_COMMENTS_REASONS_TO_EXCLUDE = {\"abuse\", \"off-topic\", \"duplicate\", \"spam\"}` 和 `if (comment.minimizedReason not in MINIMIZED_COMMENTS_REASONS_TO_EXCLUDE and comment.author):`",
    "advice": "建议验证 GitHub GraphQL API 返回的 `minimizedReason` 字段的确切值。根据 GitHub 文档，可能的值是 `\"ABUSE\"`、`\"OFF_TOPIC\"`、`\"DUPLICATE\"`、`\"SPAM\"`、`\"OUTDATED\"`、`\"RESOLVED\"` 等。因此，应将 `MINIMIZED_COMMENTS_REASONS_TO_EXCLUDE` 集合中的值更新为与 API 返回的值匹配（例如，使用大写和下划线）。或者，在比较之前，将 `minimizedReason` 转换为小写并替换下划线为连字符，以确保一致性。",

    "evidence": "`LowerStr = Annotated[str, BeforeValidator(lambda v: v.lower() if isinstance(v, str) else v)]` 和 `class DiscussionsCommentsNode(CommentsNode): minimizedReason: LowerStr | None = None`",
    "advice": "考虑将 `LowerStr` 重命名为更具描述性的名称，例如 `LowercaseStr` 或 `CaseInsensitiveStr`，并在其定义处添加注释，说明其用途。此外，检查 `DiscussionsCommentsNode` 是否真的需要重新声明 `minimizedReason` 字段，因为它可能从 `CommentsNode` 继承该字段。如果继承的字段类型相同，则无需重新声明，除非需要覆盖默认值或添加其他验证。",

review:
[
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "代码变更将函数参数 `q` 的默认值从 `None` 改为 `...` (Ellipsis)。在 FastAPI 中，`...` 用于表示一个参数是必需的，即使其类型注解允许 `None`。然而，此变更与函数内部的逻辑冲突。函数内部检查 `if q:`，这意味着当 `q` 为 `None` 时，`results.update({"q": q})` 不会执行。如果 `q` 被设置为 `...`，它将成为必需的参数，但类型 `str | None` 表明它可以接受 `None`。这会导致一个矛盾：API 调用者必须提供一个值（即使是空字符串或 `null`），但函数逻辑期望 `q` 可能为 `None` 以跳过更新结果。这破坏了原有的可选查询参数行为，可能导致 API 行为不符合预期（例如，调用者必须显式传递 `q`，但传递 `null` 可能被函数逻辑忽略）。",
    "evidence": "变更行: `async def read_items(q: Annotated[str | None, Query(min_length=3)] = ...):`。函数内部逻辑: `if q: results.update({\"q\": q})`。",
    "advice": "恢复原来的默认值 `None`，以保持 `q` 作为可选查询参数的行为，这与函数内部逻辑和项目文档（关于可选参数）一致。如果意图是使参数必需但允许 `None` 值，应重新考虑设计，可能需要调整内部逻辑或使用不同的类型提示。",
    "confidence": 0.95,
    "severity": "High"
  },
  {
    "type": "Design",
    "description": "代码变更违反了 FastAPI 项目关于可选参数声明的设计一致性。在 FastAPI 中，使用 `= None` 作为默认值是声明可选参数的惯用方式，而 `= ...` 用于声明必需参数。此变更将一个原本可选（通过 `= None` 和 `if q:` 逻辑体现）的参数改为必需（`= ...`），这与项目内大量示例和设计模式不一致。提交信息提到“Fix documentation for required can be None parameter”，但此代码变更本身可能引入了设计上的混淆，因为它混合了“必需”的声明与“可为 None”的类型以及处理 `None` 的内部逻辑。",
    "evidence": "变更行: `async def read_items(q: Annotated[str | None, Query(min_length=3)] = ...):`。项目背景中强调“Short: Minimize code duplication. Multiple features from each parameter declaration.” 和 “Easy: Designed to be easy to use and learn.”。不一致的参数声明模式会破坏这些目标。",
    "advice": "遵循 FastAPI 的既定设计模式：如果参数是真正可选的（即调用者可以完全省略），应使用 `= None` 作为默认值。如果参数是必需的但允许 `None` 值，应确保内部逻辑正确处理 `None`，并且考虑这是否是预期的 API 设计。建议审查此变更的意图，并可能调整相关文档而非代码。",
    "confidence": 0.9,
    "severity": "Medium"
  }
]
